query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "masking stale observations helps search agents -- until it doesn't: a " linguistics
rationale: The paper investigates the internal learning dynamics and factual recall mechanisms of language models during pre-training, which is distinct from the user's specific question regarding the "Masking Stale Observations" technique in search agents (a reinforcement learning/search context). While both involve LLMs, the paper does not measure the same mechanism (search agent observation masking), address the same variables (stale vs. fresh observations in a search trajectory), or provide a baseline
